reasoning_chain: The arid, semi-desert landscape with sparse vegetation and distant mountains is characteristic of northern Chile’s Atacama Desert region.
The unpaved gravel road with no center line or markings suggests a remote rural area common in Chile’s less developed zones.
Fencing along the roadside is typical for livestock management in Chilean agricultural or pastoral regions.
